record.field: Typecho is a lightweight, self-hosted PHP blogging platform that delivers a complete CMS in a remarkably small footprint.

Typecho is a lightweight, self-hosted PHP blogging platform that delivers a complete CMS in a remarkably small footprint.

Built around clean, minimal code and native Markdown support, Typecho gives writers and developers a fast, hackable blog engine without the bloat of larger platforms. It runs on common LAMP/LEMP stacks, supports multiple databases, and offers a full plugin and theme system for extending the site. It is well suited to people who want a simple, performant blog they fully control.

Key features
  • Native Markdown writing and clean, fast publishing workflow
  • Multi-database support: MySQL/MariaDB, SQLite and PostgreSQL
  • Extensible plugin and theme architecture with a mature ecosystem
  • Very low resource usage thanks to a compact, efficient codebase
  • Custom fields, pages and post types for flexible content
  • Simple installation on standard PHP hosting and easy migration
Best for

Individuals, developers and small teams who want a fast, minimal, fully self-hosted blog or content site with low server requirements and a clean publishing experience.

services: typecho: image: 80x86/typecho:latest restart: unless-stopped environment: PHP_TZ: Asia/Shanghai PHP_MAX_EXECUTION_TIME: 600 volumes: - ./typecho_data:/data depends_on: - db container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:80' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network db: image: mysql:5.7 restart: unless-stopped environment: MYSQL_DATABASE: typecho MYSQL_USER: typecho MYSQL_PASSWORD: typecho_password MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: root_password volumes: - ./db_data:/var/lib/mysql container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump de la base de APLICACIÓN. Clave: usa MYSQL_PWD (env), NUNCA `-p` — con `-p` y # password vacío (apps con MYSQL_RANDOM_ROOT_PASSWORD) mysql espera un prompt por stdin # y CUELGA. Prefiere el usuario de app (MYSQL_USER/PASSWORD/DATABASE); cae a root solo si # no hay usuario de app (excluye schemas de sistema, que romperían la auth al restaurar). docker compose exec -T db sh -c ' # MariaDB 11+ renombró mysqldump→mariadb-dump y mysql→mariadb: detectamos el binario. DUMP=$(command -v mysqldump || command -v mariadb-dump); CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_DATABASE:-}" ]; then export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD" exec "$DUMP" -u"$MYSQL_USER" --single-transaction --databases "$MYSQL_DATABASE" fi export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}" DBS=$("$CLI" -uroot -N -e "SHOW DATABASES" | grep -Ev "^(mysql|sys|information_schema|performance_schema)$") [ -z "$DBS" ] && { echo "REVISAR: sin bases de aplicacion para respaldar" >&2; exit 0; } exec "$DUMP" -uroot --single-transaction --databases $DBS ' > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "mysqldump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d db # Readiness + restore en UN exec (credenciales DENTRO del contenedor, con MYSQL_PWD → # jamás cuelga esperando un prompt de password). Prefiere el usuario de app; cae a root. docker compose exec -T db sh -c ' CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then U="$MYSQL_USER"; export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD"; else U=root; export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}"; fi for i in $(seq 1 60); do "$CLI" -u"$U" -e "SELECT 1" >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 2; done exec "$CLI" -u"$U" ' < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "restore MySQL OK"
